Title: Defining essential processes in plant pathogenesis with Pseudomonas syringae pv. tomato DC3000 disarmed polymutants and a subset of key type III effectors

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes bacterial growth and disease development by creating an aqueous apoplast
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HopM1 is one of the key effectors sufficient for basic pathogenicity in combination with AvrPtoB. 2. HopM1 redundantly promotes an aqueous apoplastic environment with AvrE1. 3. HopM1 targets MIN7, suppresses ROS burst, stomatal closure and callose deposition, and promotes water soaking and necrotic symptoms.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: deletion/reassembly in DC3000 polymutants; restoration to D28E; host mutant phenocopy using min7 Arabidopsis; symptom and bacterial growth assays
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: high humidity; dip inoculation; pressure infiltration
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes apoplastic water soaking and bacterial growth; suppresses PTI-associated responses
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Target host susceptibility node MIN7 or related pathways to engineer resistance; monitor tradeoff because MIN7-linked trafficking affects water status and immunity
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential effects on vesicle trafficking, membrane stability and immunity networks
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Review paper; effector categorized here as Common Gene because it is neither pathway enzyme nor transcriptional regulator.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes disease by increasing apoplastic water soaking
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AvrE1 is a key effector in the HopM1/AvrE1 redundant effector group. 2. It promotes apoplastic water soaking in a MIN7-independent manner. 3. It suppresses ROS burst and callose deposition and contributes to necrotic symptoms.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: DC3000 polymutant deletion/reassembly; restoration to D28E; symptom assays in Arabidopsis and N. benthamiana
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes water soaking and suppresses PTI outputs
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential resistance engineering target through strengthening PP2A-linked PTI and vesicle trafficking pathways
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with HopM1 to promote aqueous apoplast.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Solanum lycopersicum; Arabidopsis thaliana
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: phytohormone mimic / immune signaling-associated disease symptom development
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes pathogen growth and chlorotic/necrotic symptoms by suppressing immunity
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: type III effector with kinase-interaction domains and C-terminal E3 ubiquitin ligase domain
Common_Genes.Interacting_Proteins: FLS2; BAK1; CERK1/Bti9; Pto; Fen; MKK2
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AvrPtoB is one of the two minimal effectors sufficient with HopM1 for robust chlorotic spot formation and substantial growth in N. benthamiana. 2. It redundantly blocks early immune responses with AvrPto. 3. It suppresses PTI and ETI through multiple domains and can suppress cell death elicited by HopAD1.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: deletion/reassembly in DC3000 polymutants; domain mutant analysis; symptom assays; interaction studies summarized in review
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: suppresses PTI/ETI and promotes chlorosis, necrosis and bacterial growth
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Deploy or stack PRRs/NLRs that evade AvrPtoB suppression; consider E3-domain polymorphism in pathogen surveillance
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: A decisive minimal-pathogenesis effector with modular domains.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Latin_Name: Nicotiana benthamiana; Solanum lycopersicum; Arabidopsis thaliana
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: promotes pathogenesis by blocking PTI perception
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. AvrPto is a key effector in the AvrPto/AvrPtoB redundant effector group. 2. It inhibits PRR-associated kinase signaling and suppresses PTI perception. 3. In some hosts it contributes to ethylene-dependent necrotic symptoms and can elicit ETI.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: deletion mutants; restoration to polymutants; transcriptional profiling summarized in review
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: suppresses PTI perception and supports virulence
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Use PRR diversification or decoy/NLR deployment to counter AvrPto-mediated suppression
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Acts redundantly with AvrPtoB; not alone highlighted as sufficient minimal pair in N. benthamiana.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: contributes to disease by reducing extracellular immunity
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HopE1 emerged from reassembly screens as an additional key effector for near wild-type growth or symptom development. 2. It reduces cell wall-based extracellular immunity. 3. It dissociates MAP65 from microtubules in the presence of calmodulin and reduces secretion of defense proteins.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: shuffled reassembly screen; transgenic expression studies summarized in review
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: reduces extracellular immunity and contributes to symptom development
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential resistance target via safeguarding secretion and microtubule-associated defense processes
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Key but not sufficient minimal effector.

Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HopG1 emerged from reassembly screens as a key additional effector for symptom development. 2. It promotes chlorotic symptoms. 3. It affects actin remodeling through mitochondria-associated interactions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: shuffled reassembly screen; localization and phenotypic assays summarized in review
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes chlorosis and contributes to virulence
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential target for strengthening organelle-associated immune resilience
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Impacts actin remodeling rather than nutrient metabolism.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: contributes to necrosis, osmotic stress modulation and meristem chlorosis
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. HopAM1 is one of the three additional key effectors identified in the reassembly screen. 2. It contributes to necrosis and meristem chlorosis and is linked to ABA signaling and osmotic stress. 3. It may promote apoplastic water availability during drought stress.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: shuffled reassembly screen; accession-dependent cell death assays summarized in review
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: promotes necrosis/chlorosis and modulates osmotic stress-related susceptibility
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Potential resistance target through ABA/water-stress signaling pathways; caution due to pleiotropy in drought responses
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Manipulating ABA-linked pathways may affect drought tolerance and development
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Mode of action remains poorly understood.

Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: bacterial product acting in infected leaf tissue
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: reverses stomatal closure and promotes chlorotic and necrotic disease symptoms
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. cmaL encodes a previously unknown step in coronamic acid production. 2. Loss of cmaL in D28E abolished full coronatine production, leaving only CFA production. 3. Restoring cmaL enables COR production and strongly enhances chlorotic spot formation and disease symptoms.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: gene deletion/restoration in D28E; metabolite-based genetic inference from CFA/CMA/COR phenotypes; symptom assays
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: enables coronatine biosynthesis and increases chlorosis and virulence
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful target for reducing coronatine-mediated virulence in pathogen control strategies
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because coronatine is a metabolite directly discussed; gene is not a plant nutrient metabolism gene.
